title: Christmas Curried Fish
excerpt: We know curried fish as a dish you eat around Easter time. Over the years I've realised that curried fish in your fridge over the Christmas season is a dish you can feed unexpected guests with. It's lovely to eat in the warm summer months. YOU NEED: 1 kg …
html:
We know curried fish as a dish you eat around Easter time. Over the years I've realised that curried fish in your fridge over the Christmas season is a dish you can feed unexpected guests with. It's lovely to eat in the warm summer months.

 

YOU NEED:

  • 1 kg hake/stockfish
  • fish spice (optional, can be replaced with salt and pepper)
  • 4 onions cut into rings
  • 30 ml oil
  • 60 ml pickling spice
  • 5 ml chilli flakes
  • 30 ml turmeric
  • 60 ml curry powder
  • 10 ml salt
  • 5 ml pepper
  • 750 ml brown vinegar
  • 250 ml water
  • 200 ml sugar
  • 4 bruised lemon or bay leaves

NOW:

  1. cut the fish into pieces and pack it in an oven dish
  2. sprinkle it with fish spice or salt and pepper
  3. bake the fish for 20 minutes at 180 degrees (I put my fish in frozen) – the fish should be cooked but firm.
  4. fry the onions and pickling spice in oil until glassy, not brown
  5. mix the rest of the ingredients in a bowl and add to the onions in the pot – cook for 15 minutes
  6. now pack layers of fish and layers of curry mixture in a plastic container
  7. leave in the fridge for at least 2 days – if you can hold out that long

Enjoy the fish with a salad or a thick slice of warm bread. It's a winner in your fridge and just gets better the longer the fish sits in the sauce. You can use any firm white fish for this dish.
